behaviors.description: The request sent to the external API always includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels.
behaviors.justification:
MOCK-MISS: failure is at module handoff (taxonomy source + request builder + API client payload), not a single pure function. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network needed; a stub transport can capture payload exactly. LLM-DEP: no model-output judgment required. MULTI-STEP: no.
Slot A (evidence): "request sent to the external API always includes the complete list".
Slot B (boundary): local module-to-client payload boundary.
Slot C (adjacent-lower miss-proof): Unit on only formatter logic can pass while wiring drops/overwrites labels before send.
Final label: Integration because Slot B is cross-module payload wiring and Slot C shows Unit can miss the real failure.
Rejection-gate: System rejected by text "request ... includes ... labels" (payload content check can be done with in-process stub, no real network behavior needed).
behaviors.test_description: Wire real request-construction path with a stub API client; assert outbound payload contains every taxonomy label for multiple taxonomies/configs, including edge cases (ordering/whitespace).

behaviors.description: When the API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy, the module returns "unknown" instead of forwarding the unexpected value to the caller.
behaviors.justification:
MOCK-MISS: failure appears when API response parsing + taxonomy validation + return mapping interact. REAL-INFRA: no real network required; stub response is sufficient. LLM-DEP: correctness is deterministic mapping, not model quality. MULTI-STEP: no.
Slot A (evidence): "When the API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy".
Slot B (boundary): response adapter -> validator -> public return boundary.
Slot C (adjacent-lower miss-proof): Unit of validator alone can pass while integration forwards raw label due to missed call/order bug.
Final label: Integration from Slot B and Slot C.
Rejection-gate: Agentic rejected by text "returns \"unknown\"" for out-of-taxonomy value; this is wrapper logic independent of model judgment quality.
behaviors.test_description: Run module end-to-end with stubbed API returning invalid labels (case variants, extra punctuation, unseen values) and assert caller always receives "unknown".

behaviors.description: For identical raw API responses, the module's normalization step always produces the same final label.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: behavior is one local normalization function/object on identical input. REAL-INFRA: none. MOCK-HIDE: none; fakes do not hide this deterministic property. LLM-DEP: none (input is fixed raw response). MULTI-STEP: no. Rejection-gate: Integration rejected by text "identical raw API responses" and explicit focus on "normalization step" (pure deterministic transform).
behaviors.test_description: Property-style unit test for normalization: feed the same raw response repeatedly (and equivalent formatting variants) and assert identical normalized output each run.

behaviors.description: On repeated live API calls for the same document and taxonomy, the returned label remains consistent unless the provider response itself changes.
behaviors.justification:
MOCK-MISS: stubs cannot expose provider/model variability. REAL-INFRA: yes (live API), but core risk is model decision behavior across runs. LLM-DEP: explicit dependence on real model outputs over repeated calls. MULTI-STEP: repeated independent calls, not chained agent pipeline.
Slot A (evidence): "repeated live API calls" and "label remains consistent unless the provider response itself changes".
Slot B (boundary): real AI provider inference outcome boundary.
Slot C (adjacent-lower miss-proof): System test with fixed canned responses can pass while real model drifts/varies and breaks consistency expectation.
Final label: Agentic because Slot B is model-output dependent and Slot C shows lower levels miss true failures.
Rejection-gate: Workflow rejected since behavior is not a multi-stage agent chain; it is repeated single-step classification calls.
behaviors.test_description: Call real provider multiple times with identical document/taxonomy under controlled settings; record raw responses and final labels, and assert label stability except where raw provider output differs.

behaviors.description: Request construction enforces the configured token budget before dispatch (for example truncate/reject according to policy).
behaviors.justification:
MOCK-MISS: failure often occurs at config -> token estimator -> policy -> dispatch gating interaction. REAL-INFRA: no real network required; dispatch can be stubbed. LLM-DEP: no model judgment needed. MULTI-STEP: no.
Slot A (evidence): "before dispatch" and "truncate/reject according to policy".
Slot B (boundary): internal policy engine handoff to dispatch layer.
Slot C (adjacent-lower miss-proof): Unit of truncation function can pass while caller still dispatches over-budget payload due to missed gate wiring.
Final label: Integration from Slot B and Slot C.
Rejection-gate: System rejected because behavior is pre-dispatch local enforcement; exact failure can be reproduced with in-process wiring and stub sender.
behaviors.test_description: Use real module wiring with stub sender; vary input lengths and budget config, then assert truncate/reject policy is applied and over-budget requests are not dispatched.

behaviors.description: Provider-reported token usage for a typical document stays within the configured per-call budget.
behaviors.justification:
MOCK-MISS: mocked usage metadata can mask real provider accounting/model-output-driven token usage. REAL-INFRA: live API needed. LLM-DEP: execution passes through real AI API and observed token usage can depend on actual model response behavior. MULTI-STEP: no.
Slot A (evidence): "Provider-reported token usage" and "stays within ... budget".
Slot B (boundary): real provider metering/response boundary.
Slot C (adjacent-lower miss-proof): System/Integration with synthetic usage fields can always satisfy budget while production provider reports higher usage.
Final label: Agentic because Slot B is real AI-provider output/metadata and Slot C shows mocked lower levels miss it.
Rejection-gate: Workflow rejected; only one model call is evaluated, no chained agentic stages.
behaviors.test_description: Run live classification calls for representative documents; assert provider-reported prompt+completion token totals remain <= configured budget, and fail on any over-budget observation.

behaviors.description: When the API signals that the request rate is too high, the module waits and retries rather than returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification:
MOCK-MISS: bug appears in retry policy + backoff timer + client wrapper interaction. REAL-INFRA: real network/rate-limit service not required; deterministic stub 429/Retry-After plus controllable clock can reproduce. LLM-DEP: no dependence on model content. MULTI-STEP: retry loop is control-flow, not multi-agent reasoning.
Slot A (evidence): "API signals ... rate is too high" and module "waits and retries".
Slot B (boundary): API error adapter -> retry scheduler -> caller return boundary.
Slot C (adjacent-lower miss-proof): Unit test of backoff calculator can pass while integration fails to retry due to incorrect exception mapping or timer wiring.
Final label: Integration from Slot B and Slot C.
Rejection-gate: System rejected because the failure can be triggered with in-process stubbed responses and fake time; no OS/network primitive is uniquely required.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test with stub API returning rate-limit response sequence (e.g., 429 then success), fake clock/backoff control, and assertions that module delays, retries, and ultimately returns successful label.
